title: Linux - Tổng hợp câu lệnh Linux
content:
Linux - df / du / fsck / e2fsck / tune2fs / dumpe2fs / XFS / ReiserFS

◆ Managing file systems

File system free space and inode usage aredfYou can check it with the command. In Linux, the following two are
It is stored separately. (2) is stored ininodeIt is an administrative area called . inode is the file system
It is automatically prepared when creating files and directories and is used one by one when creating. to disk space
Even if there is space, if a large number of small files are generated and there are insufficient inodes, new files cannot be generated.

(1) File data
(2) File attributes and management information

◆ Syntax : df [ Options ] [ Device Name / Directory ]

option explanation
-i View inode usage
-h Displays capacity in units (MB and GB)
-k Capacity displayed in KB
-m Capacity displayed in MB 　

　

To view the capacity of a directory or file, clickducommand. Directory name in the argument
If not displayed, the current directory is displayed.

◆ Syntax : du [ Option ] [ Device Name / Directory ]

option explanation
-a Displays not only directories, but also file capacity
-c Total capacity is also displayed
-h Convert capacity to units (MB or GB)
-k Capacity displayed in KB
-m Capacity displayed in MB
-s Show totals for only specified files and directories
-S Show totals without including capacity in subdirectories

　

【 Difference between df and du 】
command meaning explanation
df DiskFree Viewing file system free space
du DiskUsed View how much space files in a directory are using

◆ Checking and repairing file systems

If a file system failure causes a system failure,fsckThe command allows the file system to
You can attempt to check the integrity and repair the failure. Disk Check and Repair File System
in the unmounted state. * If you execute it on a disk that is being written, the file system may be corrupted.

◆ Syntax: fsck [ option ] device name

option explanation
-t Specifying the file system type (ext2, ext3, xfs, etc.)
-a Perform repairs automatically
-r Perform remediation interactively
-A Checking all filesystems listed in /etc/fstab
-N Shows what the command does without actually repairing

◆ Execution example: Check /dev/sda1 in ext3 file system
　# fsck -t ext3 -a /dev/sda1

The ext2, ext3, and ext4 file systems use the e2fsck command to check and repair file systems.

◆ Syntax: e2fsck [ option ] device name

option explanation
-c Checking for bad blocks
-p Automatically repairs all bad blocks
-v Show more information
-y Automatically answer “yes” to all inquiries
-n Automatically answer “no” to all inquiries

◆ Execution example: Check /dev/sda1 in ext3 file system
　# e2fsck -y /dev/sda1

◆ File system tuning

tune2fsThe command allows you to set various parameters of ext2, ext3, and ext4 file systems.
In addition to setting parameters, you can also convert file systems from ext2 format to ext3 format.

◆ Syntax: tune2fs [ option ] device name

option explanation
-c count Specify the maximum number of times a file system can be mounted without checking it
-i time Specifying the File System Check Interval

  • j Converting a file system from ext2 format to ext3 format
  • L Setting the volume label for a file system

◆ Execution example: Converting the /dev/sda5 file system from ext2 format to ext3 format
　# tune2fs -j /dev/sda5

dumpe2fsThe command displays various information about ext2, ext3, and ext4 file systems.

◆ Syntax : dumpe2fs [ option ] device name

◆ Execution example: Display the contents of the superblock of /dev/sda1
　# dumpe2fs -h /dev/sda1

◆ XFS and ReiserFS File Systems

Although many Linux distributions now have ext3 or ext4 as the standard file system,
XFS and ReiserFSFile system is also available. Each file system has its own commands.

File System explanation
XFS A file system developed by SGI. Suitable for handling large files.
ReiserFS A journaling file system developed for Linux. Fast file search.

【 Main commands used in XFS file system 】
command explanation
mkfs.xfs Creating an XFS file system (command invoked with mkfs -t)
xfs_info View XFS file system information
xfs_db Perform XFS file system debugging
xfs_check Check XFS file system
xfsdump XFS File System Backup

【 Main commands used in ReiserFS file system 】
command explanation
mkfs.reiserfs Creating a ReiserFS file system (mkreiserfs, same for mkfs -t reiserfs)
reiserfsck ReiserFS File System Checks
debugreiserfs Troubleshooting ReiserFS File System Problems
reiserfstune Adjust ReiserFS file system parameters
